children.name: Allothunnus
children.description: Allothunnus — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Allothunnus fallai
children.description: The slender tuna, Allothunnus fallai, is a species of tuna, the only species in the genus Allothunnus, found around the world in the southern oceans between latitudes 20° and 50° South, although there are two records of probable vagrants, one in Los Angeles Harbour and the other from the North Pacific subarctic gyre. It has a more elongated body than other species of tuna with which it is symaptric such as the albacore The colour is blue-black on the back with silvery greyish-white sides, however some individuals have a coppery sheen soon after capture. It has a small second dorsal and anal fins resembling a small albacore, but the slender tuna lacks the long sweeping pectoral fins characteristic of albacores.

children.name: Katsuwonus
children.description: Katsuwonus — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Katsuwonus pelamis
children.description: The skipjack tuna is a perciform fish in the tuna family, Scombridae, and is the only member of the genus Katsuwonus. It is a cosmopolitan pelagic fish found in tropical and warm-temperate waters. Skipjack tuna can grow up to 1.1 m in length, and is a very important species for fisheries.

children.name: Katsuwonus sp.85-5R
children.description: Katsuwonus sp.85-5R — a species in the genus Katsuwonus, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Auxis
children.description: Auxis — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Auxis rochei
children.description: The bullet tuna is a species of tuna, in the family Scombridae, found circumglobally in tropical oceans, including the Mediterranean Sea, in open surface waters to depths of 50 m (164 ft). The population of bullet tuna in the Eastern Pacific was classified as a subspecies of A. rochei eudorax, but some authorities regard this as a valid species Auxis eudorax.

children.name: Auxis sp.209-10R
children.description: Auxis sp.209-10R — a species in the genus Auxis, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Auxis spec.
children.description: Auxis spec. — a species in the genus Auxis, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Auxis tapeinosoma
children.description: Auxis tapeinosoma — a species in the genus Auxis, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Auxis thazard
children.description: The frigate tuna, frigate mackerel or alagaduwa is a species of tuna, in the family Scombridae, found around the world in tropical oceans. The eastern Pacific population is now regarded as a separate species by some authorities, Auxis brachydorax.

children.name: Thunnus
children.description: Thunnus — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Thunnus alalunga
children.description: The albacore, known also as the albicore or longfin tuna, is a species of tuna of the order Scombriformes. It lives in temperate and tropical waters across the globe in the epipelagic and mesopelagic zones. There are six distinct stocks known globally in the Atlantic, Pacific, and Indian oceans, as well as in the Mediterranean Sea.

children.name: Thunnus albacares
children.description: The yellowfin tuna is a species of tuna found in pelagic waters of tropical and subtropical oceans worldwide.

children.name: Thunnus atlanticus
children.description: The blackfin tuna is a species of tuna in the family Scombridae. It is occasionally referred to as the Bermuda tuna, blackfinned albacore, or deep bodied tunny. They are the smallest tuna species in the genus Thunnus, generally growing to a maximum of 100 cm (39 in) in length and weighing 21 kg (46 lb).

children.name: Thunnus obesus
children.description: The bigeye tuna is a species of true tuna of the genus Thunnus, belonging to the wider mackerel family Scombridae. Bigeye tuna are found in the open waters of all tropical and temperate oceans, but not in the Mediterranean Sea.

children.name: Thunnus orientalis
children.description: The Pacific bluefin tuna is a predatory species of tuna found widely in the northern Pacific Ocean, but it is migratory and also recorded as a visitor to the south Pacific.

children.name: Thunnus sp. BOLD:AAA7352
children.description: Thunnus sp. BOLD:AAA7352 — a species in the genus Thunnus, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Thunnus thynnus
children.description: The Atlantic bluefin tuna is a species of tuna in the family Scombridae. It is variously known as the northern bluefin tuna, giant bluefin tuna, and formerly as the tunny.

children.name: Thunnus tonggol
children.description: Thunnus tonggol is a species of tuna of tropical Indo-West Pacific waters.

children.name: Euthynnus
children.description: Euthynnus — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Euthynnus affinis
children.description: thumb|Global capture production of Kawakawa (Euthynnus affinis) in thousand tonnes from 1950 to 2022, as reported by the [[Food and Agriculture Organization|FAO ]] Euthynnus affinis, the mackerel tuna, little tuna, eastern little tuna, wavyback skipjack tuna, kawakawa, or tongkol komo is a species of ray-finned bony fish in the family Scombridae, or mackerel family.

children.name: Euthynnus lineatus
children.description: Euthynnus lineatus, the black skipjack tuna or black skipjack, is a species of ray-finned bony fish in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Euthynnus sp. BOLD:AAB2798
children.description: Euthynnus sp. BOLD:AAB2798 — a species in the genus Euthynnus, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Gymnosarda
children.description: Gymnosarda — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Cybiosarda
children.description: Cybiosarda — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Cybiosarda elegans
children.description: The leaping bonito (Cybiosarda elegans) is a species of saltwater finfish from the Scombridae (Mackerel) family.

children.name: Sarda
children.description: Sarda — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Sarda chiliensis
children.description: Sarda chiliensis, the eastern Pacific bonito, is a marine species of bonito.

children.name: Sarda sarda
children.description: The Atlantic bonito is a large mackerel-like fish of the family Scombridae. It is common in shallow waters of the Atlantic Ocean, the Mediterranean Sea, and the Black Sea, where it is an important commercial and game fish.

children.name: Sarda sp.Tfr45b
children.description: Sarda sp.Tfr45b — a species in the genus Sarda, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Grammatorcynus
children.description: Grammatorcynus — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Scomberomorus
children.description: Scomberomorus — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Scomberomorus cavalla
children.description: The king mackerel surmayi or kingfish, is a migratory species of mackerel of the western Atlantic Ocean and Gulf of Mexico. It is an important species to both the commercial and recreational fishing industries.

children.name: Scomberomorus maculatus
children.description: The Atlantic Spanish mackerel is a migratory species of mackerel that swims to the northern Gulf of Mexico in spring, and returns to southern Florida in the eastern Gulf, and to Mexico in the western Gulf in the fall.

children.name: Scomberomorus munroi x Scomberomorus semifasciatus
children.description: Scomberomorus munroi x Scomberomorus semifasciatus — a species in the genus Scomberomorus, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Scomberomorus regalis
children.description: The cero, also known as the pintado, kingfish, cero mackerel, cerite or painted mackerel, is a ray-finned bony fish in the family Scombridae, better known as the mackerel family. More specifically, this fish is a member of the tribe Scomberomorini, the Spanish mackerels, and is the type species of the genus Scomberomorus. It is similar in appearance and coloration to the Atlantic Spanish mackerel, Scomberomorus maculatus, except the cero has a longitudinal stripe in addition to the spots of the Atlantic Spanish mackerel.

children.name: Scomberomorus sp. BOLD:AAB2484
children.description: Scomberomorus sp. BOLD:AAB2484 — a species in the genus Scomberomorus, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Acanthocybium solandri
children.description: The wahoo is a scombrid fish found worldwide in tropical and subtropical seas. In Hawaii, the wahoo is known as ono. The species is sometimes called hoo in the United States.

children.name: Acanthocybium sp. BOLD:AAH9021
children.description: Acanthocybium sp. BOLD:AAH9021 — a species in the genus Acanthocybium, family Scombridae. Also known as tunas and mackerels.

children.name: Rastrelliger kanagurta
children.description:
The Indian mackerel or bigmouth mackerel is a species of mackerel in the family Scombridae. It is commonly found in the Indian and West
Pacific oceans, and their surrounding seas. It is an important food fish and is commonly used in South and South-East Asian cuisine.

children.description: Scomber — a genus of tunas and mackerels in the family Scombridae.

children.name: Scomber japonicus
children.description: The chub mackerel, Pacific mackerel, or Pacific chub mackerel is a species of fish in the tuna and mackerel family, Scombridae. This species of mackerel closely resembles the Atlantic chub mackerel.
